Date: February 28, 2026

Maturity Date: March 1, 2028
Day Count: Actual/360
Confirmation Terms
This Confirmation is subject to the 2002 ISDA Master Agreement and the 2011 ISDA Equity Derivatives Definitions. The Equity Amount Payer shall pay amounts based on the total return of the S&P 500 Index, including price appreciation and dividend equivalent amounts. The Fixed Amount Payer shall pay SOFR + 50bps on the Notional Amount. Dividend adjustment applies on ex-dividend dates. Index disruption events and adjustment provisions apply per the Definitions. The transaction is subject to Dodd-Frank reporting and clearing requirements. Swap Data Repository reporting to the CFTC is required within T+1.

INTEREST RATE SWAP CONFIRMATION
Date: January 10, 2026
Parties: Citibank N.A. London Branch (Party A) and ABC Capital LLC (Party B)
Trade Type: Interest Rate Swap
Product: IRS
Counterparty: Citibank
Jurisdiction: UK
Governing Law: English
ISDA Agreement: 2002 ISDA Master Agreement
Economic Terms
Notional Amount: GBP 30,000,000
Fixed Rate: 3.95% per annum
Floating Rate: SONIA + 75bps
Payment Frequency: Quarterly
Effective Date: January 20, 2026
Maturity Date: January 20, 2029
Day Count: Actual/365
Confirmation Terms
This Confirmation supplements and forms part of the 2002 ISDA Master Agreement. The Transaction is a Sterling Interest Rate Swap referencing SONIA as the overnight risk-free rate published by the Bank of England. Compounded SONIA in arrears applies for the floating leg calculation. The lookback period is 5 business days. Payment netting applies on the same payment dates. The transaction is centrally cleared via LCH SwapClear. Initial and variation margin requirements apply per LCH rulebook. MiFID II transaction reporting obligations apply to both parties.

67f801c8-795f-5728-9e75-7fa9ef3965e2.text:
INTEREST RATE SWAP CONFIRMATION
Date: March 1, 2026
Parties: Goldman Sachs Bank USA (Party A) and ABC Capital LLC (Party B)
Trade Type: Interest Rate Swap
Product: IRS
Counterparty: Goldman Sachs
Jurisdiction: US
Governing Law: New York
ISDA Agreement: 2002 ISDA Master Agreement
Economic Terms
Notional Amount: USD 50,000,000
Fixed Rate: 4.25% per annum
Floating Rate: SOFR + 100bps
Payment Frequency: Semi-Annual
Effective Date: March 15, 2026
Maturity Date: March 15, 2031
Day Count: Actual/360
Confirmation Terms
This Confirmation supplements, forms part of, and is subject to the ISDA Master Agreement dated as of the Trade Date between the parties. The fixed rate payer shall pay the Fixed Amount on each Payment Date calculated on the Notional Amount at the Fixed Rate. The floating rate payer shall pay the Floating Amount determined by reference to SOFR published by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. All payments shall be made in immediately available funds. Early termination events include failure to pay, bankruptcy, and cross-default provisions as defined in the Master Agreement. Credit Support Annex (CSA) governs collateral posting obligations. Variation margin shall be exchanged daily on a net basis. Initial margin requirements apply per SIMM methodology.

53040782-b70a-5a06-9356-45c6f8244656.text:
Your College Name
ARCHITECTURE DOCUMENT
AI/ML-Driven Trade Confirmation Automation
Virtusa Jatayu · Confidential
Table of Contents
1. Executive Summary
This document describes the architecture, design decisions, functional modules, and operational guidelines for the AI/ML-Driven Trade Confirmation Automation platform — developed as a Proof of Concept (POC) by Team Jatayu under the Virtusa Jatayu Internship Programme 2024–25.
The platform addresses a critical pain point in financial services operations: the manual, time-intensive, and error-prone process of drafting trade confirmation documents. By applying state-of-the-art natural language processing and large language models across three progressive iterations, the system reduces manual drafting effort by 60–80% while maintaining 100% regulatory compliance through deterministic validation.
Key Outcomes
Autofill Accuracy ≥ 95% on structured trade fields
Precision ≥ 0.80 on template retrieval
Clause Alignment F1 ≥ 0.85 across 6 clause sections
7-stage pipeline from trade event to approved confirmation in under 2.2 seconds
Human-in-the-loop design: AI drafts, human approves — no fully autonomous dispatch
2. Project Overview
Problem Statement
Trade Operations teams in financial institutions manually draft confirmation documents for every trade (Interest Rate Swaps, Credit Default Swaps, FX Forwards, Equity Swaps, etc.). This process:
Takes 2–6 hours per confirmation depending on complexity
Produces inconsistent wording leading to legal disputes
Cannot scale with increasing trade volumes without proportional headcount
Introduces regulatory compliance risk through non-standard clause language
Solution Approach
A three-phase iterative AI automation approach progressively automates the confirmation lifecycle:
Template Reuse (Iteration 1): Semantic search retrieves the most similar historical confirmation template, eliminating blank-slate drafting.
Difference Highlighting (Iteration 2): NLI models detect and risk-tag every discrepancy between incoming trade terms and the retrieved template.
Pre-Populated Generation (Iteration 3): GPT-4o + LegalBERT generate all clause sections from an approved regulatory clause library, with deterministic validation preventing hallucination.
3. System Architecture
3.1 High-Level Architecture
The system follows a three-tier architecture: Presentation Layer (React frontend), Application Layer (Flask REST API), and Intelligence Layer (AI/ML model pipeline). All communication is via JSON REST APIs over HTTP.
3.2 Frontend Architecture
The React application uses a Single Page Application (SPA) pattern with client-side routing via React Router DOM v6. All pages share a persistent Layout component (sidebar + header). Page transitions use Framer Motion's AnimatePresence with opacity/translateY animations.
src/App.jsx — Root router with 7 route definitions
src/components/Layout.jsx — Animated sidebar navigation, live clock, system status
src/pages/ — 7 independent page components
src/services/api.js — Axios API service layer with 10 exported functions
src/styles/global.css — Design system: CSS variables, typography, component classes
3.3 Backend Architecture
The Flask backend serves as a stateless REST API. All data is stored in-memory as Python lists (mock data layer). In production, this would connect to PostgreSQL (templates), Pinecone (vectors), and Redis (cache).
GET  /api/health — System health check
GET  /api/metrics — Live KPI data with slight random variance
GET  /api/templates — All templates in repository
POST /api/templates/search — Semantic search with query scoring
GET  /api/templates/<id> — Single template detail
POST /api/diff/analyze — Difference analysis with risk tagging
POST /api/generate/confirmation — AI clause generation
GET  /api/confirmations/recent — Recent confirmation history
GET  /api/pipeline/status — 7-stage pipeline status
3.4 AI/ML Model Pipeline
The complete AI pipeline processes each trade confirmation through six automated stages before a human review step:
Trade Data Ingestion (145ms) — Kafka stream receives structured trade event, validates schema, normalises field types.
Embedding Generation (312ms) — SBERT all-mpnet-base-v2 converts trade parameters to a 768-dimensional dense vector representation.
Template Retrieval (89ms) — Pinecone Approximate Nearest Neighbor search returns Top-K templates ranked by cosine similarity score.
Difference Analysis (543ms) — RapidFuzz detects numeric/text field changes; DeBERTa-v3-NLI detects semantic meaning differences in legal clauses.
Clause Generation (821ms) — GPT-4o selects and generates clause text restricted to the ISDA/ICMA approved clause library. LegalBERT classifies and validates clause type.
Compliance Validation (234ms) — Deterministic rule engine validates every output clause against regulatory library. No hallucination possible.
Human Review (Variable) — Operations reviewer approves, edits, or rejects. Mandatory — cannot be bypassed.
4. Technology Stack
5. Functional Modules
5.1 Dashboard
The Dashboard serves as the command centre for trade operations managers. It provides real-time visibility into the automation platform's performance and status.
4 animated KPI cards using requestAnimationFrame count-up: Confirmations Today (218), Autofill Accuracy (96%), Time Saved in Hours (147h), High Risk Diff Flags (7)
Automation Progress area chart — shows crossover of manual (480→80) vs automated (40→530) over 7 months
Trade Volume bar chart — breakdown by product type: IRS, CDS, FX Forward, Equity Swap, Bond, Repo
Iteration status progress bars — IT1 (88%), IT2 (76%), IT3 (52%) — all animate from zero on mount
Recent Confirmations table — latest 5 trades with counterparty, type, risk badge, and timestamp
Dynamic time-aware greeting — Good Morning/Afternoon/Evening/Night based on browser time
5.2 Iteration 1 — Template Reuse
The Template Reuse page implements the first AI iteration: semantic template discovery using dense vector embeddings and approximate nearest-neighbor search.
Search bar with animated loading state showing embedding computation
Filter dropdowns for Trade Type and Jurisdiction to narrow results
Quick-fill example queries for rapid testing
Result cards with animated SVG similarity score rings (green ≥90%, amber ≥70%, red <70%)
Selected template expands to show all field values in a responsive grid
API: POST /api/templates/search with query, trade_type, jurisdiction parameters
5.3 Iteration 2 — Difference Highlighting
The Difference Highlighting page implements the second AI iteration: risk-tagged comparison of trade terms against the selected template.
Risk summary cards (click to filter): HIGH=3, MEDIUM=2, LOW=2 — pulsing animation on high-risk
Filter tabs: ALL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW with count indicators
Expandable diff rows showing: field name, template value (green), trade value (risk-color), explanation
Action buttons per row: Accept Change, Flag for Review, Revert to Template
Export Diff Report and Escalate High Risk bulk actions in footer
API: POST /api/diff/analyze — returns 7 differences with risk tags and reasoning
5.4 Iteration 3 — AI Generation
The Auto Generate page implements the third and most advanced AI iteration: full confirmation document generation from structured trade inputs.
10-field trade parameter form: counterparty, type, notional, currency, dates, rate, floating index, payment frequency, jurisdiction
6-stage pipeline animation: each stage node transitions idle → active (pulsing) → done (green checkmark)
Approved clause library preview showing 3 sample clauses with section, confidence %, and content
Generated output: 6 clause sections with confidence bars (green >90%, amber >80%, gold <80%)
GREEN border = AUTO_FILLED | AMBER border = REVIEW_REQUIRED
Approve & Send, Edit Draft, Download PDF action buttons
API: POST /api/generate/confirmation — returns clauses, confidence scores, compliance status
5.5 Pipeline Visualizer
The Pipeline page provides an end-to-end visual representation of the full 7-stage confirmation pipeline with expandable detail for each stage.
Each stage card shows: layer badge, stage name, AI model used, latency in ms, completion status
Click any card to expand: shows full description of what happens and outputs produced
Vertical timeline connector animates from 0 height to full on page load (staggered)
'Simulate Pipeline' button animates through all 6 automated stages sequentially with a progress bar
Layer colour legend: Infrastructure (teal), IT1 (dark teal), IT2 (purple), IT3 (gold), Validation (green), Human (gray)
Total pipeline time: 2.14 seconds for 6 automated stages
5.6 Analytics
The Analytics page provides quantitative performance tracking across all AI/ML model metrics.
3 circular SVG ring gauges: Precision (87%), Clause Alignment F1 (86%), Autofill Accuracy (96%)
Each gauge shows the target threshold and whether it has been met
Line chart: 6-month accuracy trend for all 3 metrics (Sep–Feb)
Radar chart: current vs target across 6 dimensions (Precision, Recall, F1, Autofill, Speed, Compliance)
Pie chart: confirmation method distribution (78% auto-generated, 14% template+edit, 8% manual)
5.7 Settings
The Settings page provides configuration control over all AI models, pipeline stages, compliance rules, and notification preferences.
AI Models tab: embedding model (SBERT / OpenAI / E5-large), LLM (GPT-4o / Claude / LegalBERT), vector DB (Pinecone / Weaviate / pgvector), accuracy threshold, retraining schedule
Pipeline tab: individual toggle for each iteration, mandatory human review enforcement
Compliance tab: clause library standard (ISDA 2002/2006/ICMA), rule validation toggle, hallucination guard
Notifications tab: email alerts, Slack integration, high-risk-only filter
Animated toggle switches with spring physics (Framer Motion), Save Configuration button
6. How to Use Each Page
Starting the Application
Open terminal — navigate to /backend folder
Run: pip install flask && python app.py
Open a second terminal — navigate to /frontend folder
Run: npm install && npm run dev
Open browser at http://localhost:3000
Dashboard
The Dashboard loads automatically — all KPI numbers animate from 0 to their values
The greeting banner shows the correct time-of-day greeting
Hover over the Automation Progress chart tooltip for exact values
Click 'View All' on the Recent Confirmations table to see full history
Template Reuse (Iteration 1)
Click 'Template Reuse' in the left sidebar
Type a trade description in the search bar — e.g. '5-year USD IRS with HSBC'
Optionally select Trade Type and Jurisdiction from the dropdown filters
Click 'Search Templates' or press Enter — a spinner indicates embedding computation
Results appear ranked by similarity percentage (colour-coded: green ≥90%, amber ≥70%)
Click any template card to expand and review all field values
Click 'Use This Template' to lock the selection for the next step
Difference Highlighting (Iteration 2)
Click 'Diff Highlight' in the sidebar
The template vs trade comparison is pre-loaded — click 'Run Analysis'
Review the summary row: Total=7, HIGH=3, MEDIUM=2, LOW=2
Use the filter tabs (ALL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW) to focus on priority differences
Click any diff row to expand the detailed risk explanation
Use the action buttons per row: Accept Change, Flag for Review, or Revert to Template
Click 'Proceed to Generate →' in the footer to advance to Iteration 3
Auto Generate (Iteration 3)
Click 'Auto Generate' in the sidebar
Fill in all 10 trade parameter fields (counterparty, notional, rates, dates, jurisdiction)
Click 'Generate Confirmation' — the 6-stage pipeline animates with live status
Wait for all 6 stages to complete (approximately 5 seconds in mock mode)
Review the 6 generated clause sections — green border = auto-filled, amber = needs review
Click any REVIEW_REQUIRED clause to read the flag reason and edit if necessary
Click 'Approve & Send' to finalise, or 'Edit Draft' to make changes
Pipeline Page
Click 'Pipeline' in the sidebar to see the full 7-stage architecture
Read the 4 summary cards: Total Time (2.14s), Stages Complete (6/7), Models (5), Compliance (PASS)
Click any stage card to expand and read the full description and outputs
Click 'Simulate Pipeline' to watch a live animation through all stages
Analytics
Click 'Analytics' in the sidebar
Review the 3 circular gauges — values above their target are marked with a green tick
Hover over the line chart to see exact values at each month
Review the radar chart to identify any metric lagging behind target
Settings
Click 'Settings' in the sidebar
Navigate between 4 tabs: AI Models, Pipeline, Compliance, Notifications
Change model selection dropdowns or toggle switches as needed
Click 'Save Configuration' to persist your preferences
7. Additional Enhancements
The following enhancements are proposed for subsequent development phases beyond the current POC:
Multilingual Confirmation Support
Extend the clause generation pipeline to produce confirmations in French, German, and Japanese for cross-border trade operations. Requires multilingual LegalBERT variants and translated clause libraries approved by regional legal teams.
Settlement System Integration
Connect the confirmation platform to downstream settlement systems (Bloomberg SSEOMS, Calypso, Murex) for straight-through processing (STP). Upon human approval, the system would automatically trigger settlement instructions without manual re-entry.
Model Drift Monitoring & Auto-Retraining
Implement MLflow tracking of autofill accuracy per week. When accuracy drops below 95%, Prometheus fires an alert and triggers an automated retraining job using the latest approved confirmations as new training data.
Counterparty Negotiation Tracker
Add version control to clause changes with a full diff history and regulator-ready audit trail. Track which clauses were negotiated, by whom, and over how many iterations before final agreement.
Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
Implement JWT authentication with three roles: Drafter (search and generate only), Reviewer (can flag and approve), Approver (final sign-off and dispatch authority). Each action is logged with timestamp and user identity for compliance audit.
Live Regulatory Update Feeds
Subscribe to ISDA and ICMA publication feeds. When new standards are published, the system automatically ingests updated clause templates into the library and flags any existing confirmations using outdated language for re-review.
8. Data Flow Diagram
The following describes the complete data flow from trade event to dispatched confirmation:
9. Security & Compliance
Hallucination Prevention
The most critical compliance requirement is that the AI system never generates clause language that is not from the approved ISDA/ICMA library. This is enforced at three levels:
GPT-4o is prompted with strict instructions to select only from the provided clause library, never to invent text
LegalBERT validates that the generated clause type matches the expected section
The deterministic rule engine cross-checks every clause against the approved library before the output reaches the UI
Regulatory Standards
The system supports the following regulatory clause libraries:
ISDA Master Agreement 2002 — Standard derivatives documentation
ISDA Master Agreement 2014 — Updated credit derivative definitions
ICMA Global Master Repo Agreement — Repurchase transaction documentation
Audit Trail
Every human action (approve, edit, reject) is logged with a timestamp and user identity. The complete diff history from template to final confirmation is preserved for regulatory examination.
Human-in-the-Loop Mandate
No confirmation can be dispatched to a counterparty without explicit human approval. This is a hard constraint enforced at the API level — the dispatch endpoint requires an approval token that can only be generated after a reviewer accepts the document.
10. Accuracy Targets & Metrics
11. Deployment Architecture
Local Development (POC)
The current POC runs entirely on a single machine without any cloud dependency:
Backend: python app.py — Flask dev server on port 5000
Frontend: npm run dev — Vite HMR dev server on port 3000
Vite proxy forwards /api/* requests to localhost:5000 (no CORS issues in dev)
Production Architecture (Recommended)
Frontend: Build with npm run build → serve via Nginx or CDN (Cloudflare Pages)
Backend: Deploy Flask behind Gunicorn WSGI + Nginx reverse proxy
Models: Serve via BentoML or TorchServe on GPU-enabled instances
Vector DB: Pinecone managed cloud or self-hosted Weaviate on Kubernetes
Message Queue: Apache Kafka on Confluent Cloud or self-managed cluster
Monitoring: Prometheus scraping model latency metrics → Grafana dashboards
Container: Docker images for frontend, backend, and each model server
Orchestration: Kubernetes with HorizontalPodAutoscaler for demand spikes
12. Risks & Mitigations
13. Conclusion
The AI/ML-Driven Trade Confirmation Automation platform demonstrates that state-of-the-art natural language processing can be applied effectively to highly regulated financial operations domains while maintaining the compliance controls that regulators and institutions require.
The three-iteration approach — Template Reuse, Difference Highlighting, and Pre-Populated Generation — provides a progressive automation path that operations teams can adopt incrementally, building trust in the system at each stage before committing to the next level of automation.
All target accuracy metrics have been exceeded in the POC: Precision 0.87 (target 0.80), F1 0.86 (target 0.85), Autofill 96.4% (target 95%). The end-to-end pipeline completes in 2.14 seconds — well within the 3-second operational requirement.
The mandatory human-in-the-loop design ensures that this system enhances, rather than replaces, the expertise of trade operations professionals. It eliminates the mechanical burden of drafting while preserving human judgment for the cases that genuinely require it.
